build.title: Resume Builder
build.subtitle: Answer a few questions and we'll build a résumé from your answers.

descriptions.0: Your contact details become the résumé header. Add an optional target role.
descriptions.1: Optional — leave blank and we'll write one from your answers.
descriptions.2: Add each role, most recent first. List real achievements as bullets.
descriptions.4: List your skills — one per line. We'll group them.
descriptions.5: Optional extras: projects, publications, awards, volunteering, languages, certifications.
descriptions.6: Pick the output language and template, then build.

summary.label: Professional summary
summary.placeholder: A short paragraph about who you are and what you do. Leave blank to auto-generate.

experience.emptyDescription: Add your work history — most recent first.

education.emptyDescription: Add degrees, certifications, or relevant coursework.

projects.descriptionPlaceholder: What it does and your role.

review.incomplete: Add your name, at least one role or education entry, and some skills to build.

validation.experienceIdentity: Add a job title or company.

toast.failed: Couldn't build the résumé. Please try again.

task.strong: Strongest reasoning — best for analysis and nuanced rewrites.
task.balanced: Balanced — solid for generation; analysis is decent.
task.basic: Lightweight — fine for quick drafts; pick a larger model for deep analysis.
task.light: Fast & economical — great for generation and quick passes; deep analysis may miss nuance.

kind.local: Local · private; speed depends on your hardware.
kind.cli: CLI · uses your existing login, no API key.

applicant.title: Applicant details
applicant.description: Optional facts a résumé can't answer — used for the cover letter where a market expects them (e.g. Germany asks for a salary expectation and start date) and for auto-pilot application answers. Left blank, the app answers non-committally and never invents a number or date. Changes save automatically.

contactProfile.title: Contact Profile
contactProfile.description: The header on your résumé and cover letter is built from these fields — your name, email, phone, location, and personal profile links. Editing them keeps every generated document correct and consistent. Changes save automatically.
contactProfile.extraLinksHint: Portfolio, Dribbble, Behance, or any other personal link. Imported résumés fill these in automatically.
contactProfile.photoHint: Optional. Used by the photo templates (Portrait, Lebenslauf). Kept on your device only — never uploaded.

contactConflict.title: Update your contact details?

faq.title: Help & Support
faq.subtitle: Common problems and how to fix them.

linkedinNoResults.q: LinkedIn scraping returns no results or gets stuck
linkedinNoResults.a: Your LinkedIn session has likely expired. Fix it in order: 1. Go to Settings → Accounts and click Disconnect next to LinkedIn. 2. Click Connect — a browser window will open. Log in to your LinkedIn account manually. 3. Close the browser window once logged in, then retry scraping. If it still fails, LinkedIn may be rate-limiting your IP. Wait 15–30 minutes before trying again.

scrapingZeroJobs.q: Scraping starts but shows 0 jobs after completing
scrapingZeroJobs.a: This usually means the search returned no matching results, or LinkedIn blocked the request. Try a broader search query (e.g. "engineer" instead of "senior frontend engineer React"). Remove or relax the date filter — "Past 24 hours" on a niche query often returns 0 results. Reconnect your LinkedIn account — unauthenticated scraping returns fewer results. Try reducing Pages to 1 to test if any results come back at all.

indeedWrongCountry.q: Indeed shows jobs from the wrong country
indeedWrongCountry.a: Indeed has separate job boards per country. When scraping: 1. Select Indeed as the board in the scrape form. 2. A Region dropdown will appear — select your country (e.g. "Germany — de.indeed.com"). 3. The location field should match the selected region's language and format.

jobsDisappeared.q: Scraped jobs disappeared after I restarted the app
jobsDisappeared.a: By design, scraped jobs are ephemeral — they live in memory only and are cleared on restart. This keeps the data fresh since job listings change daily. Simply run a new scrape when you open the app. If you want to preserve which jobs you've applied to or viewed, those interactions are saved permanently in Settings → Privacy → Export Data.

clearButtonRemoved.a: The Clear button wipes the current in-memory list. Run another scrape with the same criteria to get them back. Your viewed/applied interaction history is stored separately and not affected by Clear.

aiDoesNothing.q: AI generation does nothing / spinner runs forever
aiDoesNothing.a: The AI requires Ollama to be running locally with at least one model installed. 1. Make sure Ollama is installed and running: ollama serve 2. Pull a model if you haven't: ollama pull llama3.2 3. Go to Settings → AI and select the model you pulled. 4. Try generating again.

outputToneWrong.q: Resume or cover letter output sounds wrong / off-tone
outputToneWrong.a: The AI output tone is controlled by your preference. 1. Go to Settings → AI → AI Output Tone. 2. Select the tone that matches your style (Professional, Casual, Formal, Creative). 3. Regenerate — the new tone takes effect immediately.

noRecommendations.a: Recommendations only appear when the ATS analysis finds skill gaps. Check: Both a resume and a job ad are pasted/uploaded. The job ad contains actual requirements (not just a title). An AI model is selected in Settings — without a model, the gap analysis runs but recommendations can't be generated.

browserWindowNotOpen.q: The Connect browser window doesn't open
browserWindowNotOpen.a: The browser window is a Chromium popup launched by the app. Check if it opened behind other windows — it won't show in your taskbar by default on some setups. If nothing appears after 10 seconds, try clicking Connect again. Go to Settings → Privacy → Sign Out All Accounts, then try connecting again.

linkedinGuestMode.q: I connected LinkedIn but scraping still uses guest mode
linkedinGuestMode.a: The session may not have been saved correctly. 1. Disconnect LinkedIn in Settings → Accounts. 2. Click Connect and log in again — make sure you see your LinkedIn feed before closing the window. 3. Wait 3–5 seconds after the page loads before closing. 4. Retry scraping — you should see authenticated results (more jobs, less CAPTCHAs).

interactionHistoryGone.q: My interaction history (applied/viewed jobs) is gone
interactionHistoryGone.a: This can happen if the database was cleared or the app data directory was wiped. To avoid data loss: Regularly export your data: Settings → Privacy → Export Data. To restore from a backup: Settings → Privacy → Import Data and select your export file.

appSlow.q: The app is slow or unresponsive
appSlow.a: Most slowness comes from Ollama using too much memory. Close other heavy apps while using AI generation. Use a smaller model in Settings → AI (e.g. llama3.2:1b instead of 8b). If you're not using AI, you can stop Ollama entirely — the scraping and job tracking features don't need it.

languageNotChanged.q: Language changed but some text is still in the old language
languageNotChanged.a: Some UI strings are cached. After changing the language in Settings → General, fully restart the app to apply it everywhere.

resetEverything.q: I want to reset everything and start fresh
resetEverything.a: You can selectively clear data without reinstalling: Clear interactions: Settings → Privacy → Clear Interaction History. Sign out all boards: Settings → Privacy → Sign Out All Accounts. Remove preferences: Clear your browser localStorage (open DevTools → Application → Local Storage).

networkError.q: Scraping fails with a network error
networkError.a: The app makes direct HTTP requests to job boards. Network issues can block these. Check your internet connection. Some corporate or university networks block scraping traffic — try on a different network or use a VPN. LinkedIn and Indeed occasionally block IPs temporarily after many requests. Wait 15–30 minutes and try again. Try scraping fewer pages (set Pages to 1) to reduce request volume.

captchaAppears.a: CAPTCHAs from LinkedIn/Indeed mean your IP or session has been flagged. The best fix: reconnect your LinkedIn account (authenticated requests are much less likely to trigger CAPTCHAs than guest requests). If you're already authenticated, wait 30 minutes and try scraping 1 page at a time.

diagnostics.rateLimitingIssue: Provider blocked due to rate limiting
diagnostics.rateLimitingSolution1: Wait for rate limit to reset (usually 1-24 hours)
diagnostics.captchaSolution2: Use a different account if available
diagnostics.captchaSolution4: Consider using official APIs when possible

recovery.destructiveActionsDesc: These actions cannot be undone. Use with caution.
recovery.rebuildIndexesDesc: Rebuild all vector indexes from scratch. This may take several minutes.
recovery.clearCacheDesc: Remove all cached embeddings. They will be regenerated on demand.
recovery.resetDatabaseDesc: Completely reset the vector database. All indexed data will be lost.
recovery.reloadRuntimeDesc: Restart the AI runtime and reload all models.
recovery.unloadModelsDesc: Unload all currently loaded AI models to free memory.
recovery.resetConfigDesc: Reset all model settings to defaults.
recovery.clearOcrCacheDesc: Remove all cached OCR results. Documents will be re-scanned.
recovery.reindexDocsDesc: Re-index all documents in the database.
recovery.resetSessionsDesc: Clear all scraping sessions and cookies.

aiRuntime.gpuAccelerationDesc: Running in CPU mode (GPU not detected)
aiRuntime.modelFailedToLoadInsufficientMemory: Model failed to load due to insufficient memory
aiRuntime.modelFailedToLoadSolution1: Switch to a lower-memory model (e.g., llama3:8b instead of llama3:70b)
aiRuntime.modelFailedToLoadSolution4: Close other applications to free RAM
aiRuntime.ollamaNotRespondingSolution2: Check if Ollama is running: ollama list
aiRuntime.timeoutDuringGenerationSolution4: Check network connectivity for remote models

logs.noArticlesFound: No articles found matching your criteria.
logs.exampleMessage2: Rate limit detected for LinkedIn provider

recovery.destructiveActionsDesc: These actions may delete data. Review carefully before proceeding.

contact.exportDiagnosticsBundleDesc: Export a diagnostics bundle containing system information, logs, and configuration to help troubleshoot issues.

performance.unloadInactiveModelsDesc: Free up 2.4GB by unloading models not in use

documentParsing.pdfParsingFailedSolution1: Check if PDF is password protected
documentParsing.pdfParsingFailedSolution3: Try opening in another PDF viewer
documentParsing.pdfParsingFailedSolution4: Use OCR as fallback extraction method
documentParsing.ocrConfidenceTooLowSolution1: Ensure document is scanned at 300 DPI or higher
documentParsing.ocrConfidenceTooLowSolution2: Check if text is legible and not too small
documentParsing.ocrConfidenceTooLowSolution4: Use a different OCR language model
documentParsing.emptyExtractionFromDocumentSolution1: Verify document contains text (not images only)
documentParsing.emptyExtractionFromDocumentSolution2: Check if text is embedded as images
documentParsing.emptyExtractionFromDocumentSolution3: Try OCR extraction instead of text parsing
